title: Variable diameter mounting attachment
abstract: A variable diameter mounting attachment (10) joins an accessory (300), such as a matte box, to a lens housing of a camera (200), such as a video camera, digital camera or broadcast camera, while blocking undesired light from reaching the lens. The attachment (10) incorporates two synchronized mechanisms activated together by rotation of an outer gear ring (120): (i) a series of jaws (40) to clench an outer edge of the lens housing, and (ii) a multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) to block undesired light from entering the lens. A lock lever (130) clamps the outer gear ring (120) in fixed position.
claims:
Claims 1. A mounting attachment (10) for a camera lens, comprising: a series of two or more iris diaphragm jaws (40) having a curved shape with a first end and a second end and a top surface and a bottom surface opposite the top surface, said diaphragm jaws arranged in overlapping relation to form a circular array, each diaphragm jaw having a first pin (48) extending from its top surface at or near the first end of the diaphragm jaw and having a second pin (50) extending from its opposite surface at or near the second end; a first ring (20) having a top surface and an opposite surface, with its top surface adjacent at least a portion of the top surfaces of the iris diaphragm jaws, and defining holes (26) therethrough or recesses therein each configured to receive a respective one of the first pins (48) of the diaphragm jaws; a multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) wherein each of the blades has a curved shape with a first end and a second end and a top surface and an opposite surface and the blades are arranged in overlapping relation to form a circular array, wherein each of the blades has a first pin (92) extending from its top surface at or near the first end of the blade, and wherein each of the blades has a second pin (94) extending from its bottom surface at or near the second end of the blade; a second ring (60) having a top surface and an opposite surface, with its top surface facing the series of iris diaphragm jaws and its opposite surface facing the multiple blade iris diaphragm, said second ring defining holes (66) therethrough or recesses therein each configured to receive a respective one of the first pins (92) of the blades of the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80), and said second ring defining other holes (66) therethrough or recesses therein each configured to receive a respective one of the second pins (50) of the diaphragm jaws (40), said second ring having at least one tab (70) extending radially from its outer circumference; a cover ring (100) having a front face and a rear face opposite of the front face, said cover ring having a circumferential flange (106) extending upwardly from the front face, said flange defining slots (1 10) therethrough or slot-shaped recesses therein configured to receive the tabs (70) extending from the second ring (60), said cover ring defining holes (108) or recesses in its front face with each of said holes or recesses configured to receive one of the second pins (94) of the blades of the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80); and 

 an outer gear ring (120) surrounding the circumferential flange (106) of the cover ring (100), said outer gear ring defining a center axis, said outer gear ring defining one or more slots (128) or recesses in its center axis-facing internal wall (126) each of which is configured to receive one of the at least one tabs (70) of the second ring (60), said outer gear ring configured to rotate in a first direction about the center axis and thereby to rotate the second ring (60) in the first direction to open the iris diaphragm jaws (40) to a first opening position and to open the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) to a first aperture size, said outer gear ring further configured to rotate in a second direction about the center axis and thereby to rotate the second ring (60) in the second direction to close the iris diaphragm jaws (40) to a second opening position different from the first opening position and to close the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) to a second aperture size different from the first aperture size. 2. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 1, wherein the outer gear ring (120) has ridges (122) separated by grooves on at least a portion of its outer circumferential surface. 3. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 1, wherein the outer gear ring (120) has ridges (122) separated by grooves on its entire outer circumferential surface. 4. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to one of claims 1-3, wherein each of the iris diaphragm jaws (40) have an inner curved edge with a raised rim (54) configured to seat in a groove on an external circumferential surface of a housing of the camera lens. 5. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 4, wherein the raised rim (54) comprises an elastomeric material. 6. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to one of claims 1-5, wherein the top surfaces (42) of the iris diaphragm jaws are stepped, and a portion of the bottom surface of a first iris diaphragm jaw (40) slidably contacts a portion of the stepped top surface of a second iris diaphragm jaw (40). 7. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to one of claims 1-6, wherein at least the top surfaces and opposite surfaces of the iris diaphragm jaws (40) are fabricated of a material that incorporates a friction reducing composition or are coated with a friction reducing composition. 8. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to one of claims 1-7, further comprising a lock lever (130). 9. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 8, wherein said first ring (20) further has a radially outwardly extending tab (28) configured to receive a bar (146) of the lock lever (130), and said cover ring further has a radially outwardly extending tab (28) configured to receive the bar (146) of the lock lever (130). 10. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 9, wherein the bar (146) of the lock lever (130) is connected to the tab (28) of the first ring (20) and the tab (114) of the cover ring (100). 11. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 2, further comprising a lock lever (130), wherein the lock lever has a proximal end and a distal end, wherein the distal end defines a spool opening (140) to receive a bar (146) that is connected to a radially outwardly extending tab (28) of said first ring and to a radially outwardly extending tab (114) of the cover ring (100), and wherein the lock lever has on an exterior surface of the distal end a series of ridges (138) configured to mate with the grooves (122) of the outer gear ring (120) when the lock lever is in locking configuration. 12. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 1 1, wherein the proximal end of the lock lever (130) is rotatable about an axis defined by the bar (146) to a first position to raise the series of ridges (138) of the lock lever out of contact with the grooves (122) of the outer gear ring (120), and to a second position to mate some of the ridges (138) of the series of ridges with the grooves (122) of the outer gear ring (120). 13. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to any one of claims 1-12, wherein the slots (1 10) through or slot-shaped recesses in the flange (106) of the cover ring (100) are of an arc length of from ten to thirty degrees from the center axis of the outer gear ring. 14. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 13, wherein there are four slots (1 10) through or slot-shaped recesses in the flange (106) of the cover ring (100), with each slot or slot-shaped recess spaced apart from its adjacent slot or slot-shaped recess. 15. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to any one of claims 1-14, wherein surfaces of the iris diaphragm jaws (40) have a matte black finish or other dark, non-reflective finish. 16. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to any one of claims 1-15, wherein surfaces of the blades of the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) have a matte black finish, or other dark, non-reflective finish. 17. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to any one of claims 1-16, wherein the series of iris diaphragm jaws (40) comprises three iris diaphragm jaws. 18. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to any one of claims 1-17, wherein the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) comprises twelve blades. 19. A mounting attachment (10) for a camera lens, comprising: a series of three or more jaws (40) with a first end and a second end and a top surface and a bottom surface opposite the top surface, said jaws arranged in a circular array, each jaw having a first pin (48) extending from its top surface at or near the first end of the jaw and having a second pin (50) extending from its opposite surface at or near the second end; a first ring (20) having a top surface and an opposite surface, with its top surface adjacent at least a portion of the jaws (40), and defining holes (26) therethrough or 

 recesses therein each configured to receive a respective one of the first pins (48) of the jaws (40); a multiple blade iris diaphragm (80) wherein each of the blades has a curved shape with a first end and a second end and a top surface and an opposite surface and the blades are arranged in overlapping relation to form a circular array, wherein each of the blades has a first pin (92) extending from its top surface at or near the first end of the blade, and wherein each of the blades has a second pin (94) extending from its bottom surface at or near the second end of the blade; a second ring (60) having a top surface and an opposite surface, with its top surface facing the series of jaws and its opposite surface facing the multiple blade iris diaphragm, said second ring defining holes (66) therethrough or recesses therein each configured to receive a respective one of the first pins (92) of the blades of the multiple blade iris diaphragm, and said second ring defining other holes (66) therethrough or recesses therein each configured to receive a respective one of the second pins (50) of the jaws (40), said second ring having at least one tab (70) extending radially from its outer circumference; a cover ring (100) having a front face and a rear face opposite of the front face, said cover ring having a circumferential flange (106) extending upwardly from the front face, said flange defining slots (1 10) therethrough or slot-shaped recesses therein configured to receive the tabs (70) extending from the second ring (60), said cover ring defining holes (108) or recesses in its front face with each of said holes or recesses configured to receive one of the second pins (94) of the blades of the multiple blade iris diaphragm (80); and an outer gear ring (120) surrounding the circumferential flange (106) of the cover ring (100), said outer gear ring defining a center axis, said outer gear ring defining one or more slots (128) or recesses in its center axis-facing internal wall (126) each of which is configured to receive one of the at least one tabs (70) of the second ring (60), said outer gear ring configured to rotate in a first direction about the center axis and thereby to rotate the second ring in the first direction to open the jaws to a first opening position and to open the multiple blade iris diaphragm to a first aperture size, said outer gear ring further configured to rotate in a second direction about the center axis and thereby to rotate the second ring in the second direction to close the jaws to a second opening position different from the first opening position and to close the multiple blade iris diaphragm to a second aperture size different from the first aperture size. 20. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 19, wherein each of the jaws (40) have an inner straight edge with a raised rim (54) configured to seat in a groove on an external circumferential surface of a housing of the camera lens. 21. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 20, wherein the raised rim (54) comprises an elastomeric material. 22. The mounting attachment for a camera lens according to one of claims 19-21, wherein the outer gear ring (120) has ridges (122) separated by grooves on at least a portion of its outer circumferential surface. 23. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 22, further comprising a loc lever (130), wherein the lock lever has a proximal end and a distal end, wherein the distal end defines a spool opening (140) to receive a bar (146) that is connected to a radially outwardly extending tab (28) of said first ring (20) and to a radially outwardly extending tab (1 14) of the cover ring (100), and wherein the lock lever (130) has on an exterior surface of the distal end a series of ridges (138) configured to mate with the grooves of the outer gear ring (120) when the lock lever is in locking configuration. 24. The mounting attachment for a camera lens of claim 23, wherein the proximal end of the lock lever (130) is rotatable about an axis defined by the bar (146) to a first position to raise the series of ridges (138) of the lock lever out of contact with the grooves (122) of the outer gear ring (120), and to a second position to mate some of the ridges (138) of the series of ridges with the grooves (122) of the outer gear ring.
